page: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance belonging to the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. Memory capacity is configured at 128.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This low-memory configuration is paired with 3780 GB of local storage and premium storage capabilities, though it lacks integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core performance and exceptional single-threaded compression speeds. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.76 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.91 GB/sec. Multi-core stress-ng performance is strong at 67973.49, complemented by a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 83685.33. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 4705.01 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 311.23 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For database and key-value operations, the server demonstrates high throughput, processing 109.21 million Redis SET operations per second and achieving a top-tier static web serving throughput of 87.37 GB/sec. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds across various model sizes, though text generation speeds remain average.

The D64lds_v7 presents a clear tradeoff between high-frequency compute power and memory capacity. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it is not designed for memory-intensive datasets but offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that require substantial local storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, enhances its efficiency for vector calculations and cryptography without the added expense of dedicated GPU hardware. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, compute-bound database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and premium storage capabilities. It delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 83685.33 and top-tier static web serving throughput of 87.37 GB/sec. While its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications, its advanced instruction sets provide excellent efficiency for compute-bound tasks. This instance is best suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and compute-intensive database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier web serving and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64lds_v7 features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for high-compute and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency compute instance featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs, fast local storage, and advanced instruction sets for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory paired with 3780 GB of local storage.
Top-tier performance in static web serving and single-threaded compression benchmarks.
Advanced hardware instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512.
Optimized for compute-bound databases, high-throughput web serving, and decompression.
